mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession fears, triggering sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC falls below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day forward returns in over 65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading below 3.0% confirms disinflation, prompting algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings below 3.0% are followed by negative 5-day returns in 60% of observations, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total employment and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a mean forward return above 0.1% during divergence periods over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during high inflation periods signal stagflationary pressures, triggering immediate negative market repricing within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 5-day returns in 70% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.
